Patch Set 10: Code-Review-1

I see what you are doing. You are in fact doing the opposite of the upstream patch. Due to the need to support old kernels we need to handle all the struct posix_acl_* like the older typedefs. The impression of the commit is that we are doing the opposite make using struct posix_acl_* instead.

Can you update the commit message to point out since we need to support older kernels we have to do the reverse of the upstream patch by making struct posix_acl_xattr_* behave as a typedef. This will avoid any confusion from people looking at the patch. That is my only complaint.

parents.subject: LU-9521 tests: Avoid cleanup() being called twice.

commit.subject: LU-9183 ptlrpc: handle posix_acl: xattr representation cleanups
commit.message:
LU-9183 ptlrpc: handle posix_acl: xattr representation cleanups

In commit 2211d5ba5c6c4e972ba6dbc912b2897425ea6621 typedef of
posix_acl_xattr_{header,entry} was removed and replaced with
struct posix_acl_xattr_{header,entry} declaration. Also member
a_entries was removed from struct posix_acl_xattr_header.

Since we have to support older kernels this patch does the
opposite of what was done upstream in that we treat struct
posix_acl_xattr_{header,entry} of newer kernels as the typedef
used by older kernels.

Change-Id: I880f73d495dc6c35a1671b49e509350118f6e154
Signed-off-by: Dmitry Eremin <dmitry.eremin@intel.com>
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/25892
Reviewed-by: Bob Glossman <bob.glossman@intel.com>
Tested-by: Jenkins
Reviewed-by: James Simmons <uja.ornl@yahoo.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
